records.title: feat(storefront): batch staging 20260606 — announcements, e2e, deploy CI
records.body:
## Context

`Type wijziging:` `fix` + `perf` + `ci` (staging batch)

`Scope:` announcements admin/webshop, DateTimeInput autosave, e2e staging hardening, deploy migration skip, home hydration perf

`Issue/werkplan:` staging batch `feature/batch-staging-20260606` (announcements plan + e2e/deploy follow-ups)

## Blocking Checklist

- [x] Ik werk vanuit een branch (niet direct op `main`)
- [x] Wijziging volgt bestaand pattern in deze repo
- [x] Nieuwe/gewijzigde paden hebben tests waar nodig
- [x] `npm run lint` is groen
- [x] `npm run typecheck` is groen
- [x] `npm run test` is groen
- [x] Geen `.skip`/`xit` toegevoegd
- [x] Errors blijven gestructureerd (`code`, `message`, `details`)
- [x] Geen secrets toegevoegd in code, logs of config
- [ ] Ik heb impact op `pagayo-maintenance`-smokes beoordeeld en bijgewerkt waar nodig

## Staging/Production Gate

- [x] Merge naar `main` is bedoeld voor staging-validatie
- [ ] Productie gebeurt alleen via `workflow_dispatch` met `deploy_mode=full|production-only`
- [ ] Voor productie is `target_ref` expliciet
- [ ] Voor productie is `deploy_token` vereist
- [ ] `preprod-guard` moet slagen tegen `releases/current.json`

## Verificatiebewijs

Plak hier de kernoutput (kort):

```text
lint: pass
typecheck: pass
tests: 4133 passed (workers), 2442 passed (client)
smoke (indien van toepassing): not run in this batch
```

## Risico en Rollback

`Risico:` middel — batch bevat announcements UX/API-wijzigingen (derived title, body required), e2e timing fixes, en deploy CI-gedrag.

`Rollback:` revert merge commit op `main` en redeploy staging-only van vorige `target_ref`; companion `@pagayo/design` v2.4.175 PR moet mee teruggedraaid worden voor TipTap/announcement CSS.

## Notitie voor eenpitter-flow

Human approval is niet de primaire gate. De technische gates in CI en deze checklist zijn leidend voor merge/deploy.

---

## Summary

- **Announcements:** render TipTap HTML on webshop (`RichContent` div), hide frontend title, derive admin title from message, blur-only datetime autosave, longer body debounce.
- **E2E:** retry transient staging product API during cart seed; harden guest checkout and FAQ assertions.
- **CI/deploy:** skip D1 migrations when schema unchanged.
- **Perf:** defer hero slider and contact hydration chunks on home.

**Companion:** `pagayo-design` branch `feature/batch-staging-20260606` (v2.4.175) — TipTap focus styling + announcement bar typography. Publish design tag and bump storefront lockfile before staging deploy.

## Test plan

- [ ] Admin `/admin/announcements`: edit message with H2/bold, confirm autosave after blur on schedule fields
- [ ] Pool homepage pinned banner: HTML visible, no separate title
- [ ] Staging e2e: guest checkout + FAQ specs green
- [ ] Deploy workflow: unchanged schema skips tenant migration step

records.title: perf(storefront): remove lucide-react from webshop hot-path components
records.body:
## Summary
- replace `lucide-react` imports in webshop hot-path components with local SVG icons from `src/client/icons/webshop.tsx`
- keep icon usage/appearance the same while removing icon-library coupling from first-load components
- cover hero slider, cart drawer, product grid/card, and product-list filter drawer controls

## Why
`analyze:webshop-hot-path` currently flags `vendor-icons` as part of cold-path dependencies. This change moves common storefront controls to local icons so the hot path no longer depends on lucide exports in those components.

## Changed files
- `src/client/icons/webshop.tsx`
- `src/client/features/layout/HeroSlider.tsx`
- `src/client/features/cart/CartDrawer.tsx`
- `src/client/features/product/ProductGrid.tsx`
- `src/client/features/product/ProductCard.tsx`
- `src/client/features/product/ProductList.tsx`

records.title: deps: Bump concurrently from 9.2.1 to 10.0.1
records.body:
Bumps [concurrently](https://github.com/open-cli-tools/concurrently) from 9.2.1 to 10.0.1.
Release notes

Sourced from concurrently's releases.

v10.0.1

  • Ensure FlowController type is exported - #594

Full Changelog: https://github.com/open-cli-tools/concurrently/compare/v10.0.0...v10.0.1

v10.0.0

💥 Breaking Changes

  • Dropped support for Node.js <22.0.0. Older Node.js version have reached end-of-life, and certain features require new-ish JS APIs.
  • concurrently is now ESM-only. It's now possible to require(esm). See here for interoperability.
  • Prefix colors now default to automatic - #581 The colors used to default to reset (which does nothing). Concurrently now automatically selects a color, out of the box. The list of colors used is not jarring nor carries semantic meaning, and reads well in both dark and light terminal backgrounds.
  • Removed deprecated flags and options
    • CLI flag --name-separator: use commas instead.
    • API option killOthers: use killOthersOn instead.

✨ New Features

  • Support applying modifiers to hex prefix colors (e.g. #ff0000.bold) - #450
  • Support chalk's color functions in prefixes (e.g. rgb(), hex(), bgRgb(), etc) - #578
  • Set prefix background color via bg#RRGGBB - #578
  • Allow shell override via --shell CLI flag/shell API option - #288, #589, #556 concurrently distinguishes between cmd.exe, powershell, and POSIX-based shells.
  • Manual prefix coloring in templates e.g. [{color}{name}{/color}] - #583, #587

🐛 Bug fixes

  • Scope quote normalization to CLI input - #582, #585 It should now also be possible to run commands like "/some/command" foo bar"
  • Don't throw when color doesn't exist - #580

🔐 Security

  • Address vulnerability in shellquote - #591

Other changes

  • Warn about running on Snap - #584

New Contributors

  • @​philfreo made their first contribution in open-cli-tools/concurrently#566
  • @​garretmh made their first contribution in open-cli-tools/concurrently#450
  • @​CodeF53 made their first contribution in open-cli-tools/concurrently#574
  • @​nkappler made their first contribution in open-cli-tools/concurrently#577
  • @​stephanschubert made their first contribution in open-cli-tools/concurrently#578
  • @​GermanJablo made their first contribution in open-cli-tools/concurrently#581
  • @​y-nk made their first contribution in open-cli-tools/concurrently#587
  • @​samchungy made their first contribution in open-cli-tools/concurrently#591
  • @​saito-netartz made their first contribution in open-cli-tools/concurrently#590
  • @​jeffrey-takuma made their first contribution in open-cli-tools/concurrently#585

Full Changelog: https://github.com/open-cli-tools/concurrently/compare/v9.2.1...v10.0.0

Commits
  • e70686f 10.0.1
  • a95bceb Rename flow-controller{.d -> }.ts
  • ced4245 ci: configure trusted publisher flow
  • cf2eaa2 10.0.0
  • 1b9bae4 deps: upgrade yargs to v18 (#593)
  • b05ee75 Bump min Node.js version to v22
  • ae60bc4 Scope quote normalization to CLI input (#585)
  • 2822c28 docs: update outdated Node.js doc links and fix null code formatting (#590)
  • 42f3829 deps: upgrade shell-quote to 1.8.4 (#591)
  • cb04cbf Remove deprecated flags/options
  • Additional commits viewable in compare view
Attestation changes

This version has no provenance attestation, while the previous version (9.2.1) was attested. Review the package versions before updating.

Updates `@tiptap/core` from 3.23.6 to 3.24.0
Release notes

Sourced from @​tiptap/core's releases.

v3.24.0

@​tiptap/extension-collaboration-caret

Patch Changes

  • 7bd09b7: Fix memory leak when destroying an editor while the collaboration provider stays alive (e.g. multiple editors sharing one provider). The extension's awareness update listener is now removed on destroy, so the editor can be garbage collected.
  • Updated dependencies [7bd09b7]
    • @​tiptap/pm@​3.24.0
    • @​tiptap/core@​3.24.0

@​tiptap/extension-collaboration

Patch Changes

  • 7bd09b7: Fix a memory leak where destroying an editor while its Y.Doc/provider stays alive (e.g. multiple editors sharing one provider) left the editor uncollectable. Yjs' UndoManager registered a doc.on('destroy', ...) listener that was never removed, keeping the UndoManager — and through it the whole editor — reachable from the long-lived doc. Fixed upstream in @tiptap/y-tiptap; this bumps the dependency to pull in the fix.
  • Updated dependencies [7bd09b7]
    • @​tiptap/pm@​3.24.0
    • @​tiptap/core@​3.24.0

@​tiptap/server-ai-toolkit

Patch Changes

  • 7bd09b7: Exclude toText property when serializing the schema into a JSON object, because it is a function and functions are not serializable.

@​tiptap/pm

Patch Changes

  • 7bd09b7: Add missing @tiptap/pm/inputrules export

@​tiptap/extension-details

Patch Changes

  • 7bd09b7: Fix persisted details toggles when the details node is at the start of the document.
  • Updated dependencies [7bd09b7]
    • @​tiptap/pm@​3.24.0
    • @​tiptap/core@​3.24.0
    • @​tiptap/extension-text-style@​3.24.0

@​tiptap/markdown

Patch Changes

  • 7bd09b7: Fix text inside unknown angle-bracket tags being silently swallowed during markdown parsing. HTML-like content such as <enter existing CID here if available> that does not map to any known element is now preserved as literal text instead of disappearing, which also prevents downstream contentMatchAt errors when complex schemas (Mention, Variable, Table) are in use.
  • Updated dependencies [7bd09b7]
    • @​tiptap/pm@​3.24.0
    • @​tiptap/core@​3.24.0

Updates `@tiptap/extension-link` from 3.23.6 to 3.24.0
Release notes

Sourced from @​tiptap/extension-link's releases.

v3.24.0

@​tiptap/extension-collaboration-caret

Patch Changes

  • 7bd09b7: Fix memory leak when destroying an editor while the collaboration provider stays alive (e.g. multiple editors sharing one provider). The extension's awareness update listener is now removed on destroy, so the editor can be garbage collected.
  • Updated dependencies [7bd09b7]
    • @​tiptap/pm@​3.24.0
    • @​tiptap/core@​3.24.0

@​tiptap/extension-collaboration

Patch Changes

  • 7bd09b7: Fix a memory leak where destroying an editor while its Y.Doc/provider stays alive (e.g. multiple editors sharing one provider) left the editor uncollectable. Yjs' UndoManager registered a doc.on('destroy', ...) listener that was never removed, keeping the UndoManager — and through it the whole editor — reachable from the long-lived doc. Fixed upstream in @tiptap/y-tiptap; this bumps the dependency to pull in the fix.
  • Updated dependencies [7bd09b7]
    • @​tiptap/pm@​3.24.0
    • @​tiptap/core@​3.24.0

@​tiptap/server-ai-toolkit

Patch Changes

  • 7bd09b7: Exclude toText property when serializing the schema into a JSON object, because it is a function and functions are not serializable.

@​tiptap/pm

Patch Changes

  • 7bd09b7: Add missing @tiptap/pm/inputrules export

@​tiptap/extension-details

Patch Changes

  • 7bd09b7: Fix persisted details toggles when the details node is at the start of the document.
  • Updated dependencies [7bd09b7]
    • @​tiptap/pm@​3.24.0
    • @​tiptap/core@​3.24.0
    • @​tiptap/extension-text-style@​3.24.0

@​tiptap/markdown

Patch Changes

  • 7bd09b7: Fix text inside unknown angle-bracket tags being silently swallowed during markdown parsing. HTML-like content such as <enter existing CID here if available> that does not map to any known element is now preserved as literal text instead of disappearing, which also prevents downstream contentMatchAt errors when complex schemas (Mention, Variable, Table) are in use.

Updates `date-fns` from 4.3.0 to 4.4.0
Release notes

Sourced from date-fns's releases.

v4.4.0

This release revisits the approach to CDN usage and introduces a new package, @date-fns/cdn and deprecates the date-fns CDN scripts. It allowed reducing the zipped package size from 5.83 MB down to 3.96 MB without introducing any breaking changes.

In v5.0.0-alpha.0 where CDN scripts are completely removed from date-fns the change is more significant and brings the zipped package size down to 2.89 MB.

It is just the first step in optimizing the package size. Expect further size reduction in the future v4 and v5 versions.

Changed

  • DEPRECATED: The date-fns CDN scripts are now deprecated and will be removed in the next major release. Please switch to the new @date-fns/cdn package for CDN usage.

  • Removed CDN source maps to reduce the package size. If you rely on them, please switch to the new @date-fns/cdn package that still includes them.

Updates `lint-staged` from 17.0.5 to 17.0.7
Release notes

Sourced from lint-staged's releases.

v17.0.7

Patch Changes

  • #1806 e692e58 - Update dependency tinyexec@^1.2.4.

v17.0.6

Patch Changes

  • #1803 bdf2770 - Run all tests with Deno, in addition to Node.js and Bun.

  • #1796 7508272 - Fix performance regression of lint-staged v17 by going back to using git add to stage task modifications. This was changed to git update-index --again in v17 for less manual work, but unfortunately the update-index command gets slower in very large Git repos.

  • #1797 7b2505a - This version of lint-staged uses the new staged publishing for npm packages feature. Releases are already published from GitHub Actions with trusted publishing, but now an additional approval with two-factor authentication is also required.

  • #1802 321b0a9 - Downgrade dependency tinyexec@1.2.2 to avoid issues in version 1.2.3.

Changelog

Sourced from lint-staged's changelog.

Patch Changes

17.0.6

Patch Changes

  • #1803 bdf2770 - Run all tests with Deno, in addition to Node.js and Bun.

  • #1796 7508272 - Fix performance regression of lint-staged v17 by going back to using git add to stage task modifications. This was changed to git update-index --again in v17 for less manual work, but unfortunately the update-index command gets slower in very large Git repos.

  • #1797 7b2505a - This version of lint-staged uses the new staged publishing for npm packages feature. Releases are already published from GitHub Actions with trusted publishing, but now an additional approval with two-factor authentication is also required.

records.title: feat(storefront): mobile category filter drawer, configurable card image shape + SSR flicker fixes
records.body: ## Samenvatting Net-nieuwe storefront-batch bovenop reeds gemergede #214/#216 (0 behind `main`). - **Mobiele categoriepagina's (≤768px):** facet-sidebar omgebouwd tot slide-in filter-drawer met backdrop + compacte toolbar (Filters-knop links, sort rechts, productaantal eronder). Critical CSS + scoped CSS, geen layout shift. - **SSR first-paint parity:** geëlimineerde flicker op mobiel — `facetSidebar` i18n server-side, `--stacked-sort` in SSR-header, inline `SlidersHorizontal`-icoon in SSR-skeleton, en `ensureCatalogSsrShell` behoudt category-SSR content bij hydration. - **Configureerbare product-card afbeelding (admin):** nieuwe "Afbeelding"-sectie onder category product-card settings — aspect-ratio preset (square/portrait/tall/landscape/wide), object-fit (cover/contain) + achtergrondkleur bij contain. Zod-schema, appearance-resolver met scoped CSS + `` width/height voor CLS. - **Design:** `@pagayo/design` → `2.4.161` (mobile drawer CSS), lockfile + `design-asset-version.ts` in sync. - **Test-hardening:** `isDocumentLive()`-guards op alle async `ProductList`-effects + cleanup; `setup.ts` unmount van rauwe page-mounts. Lost CI unhandled-rejection (`document is not defined`) op. ## Verificatie - CI groen op `f815a092`. - Staging-only deploy succesvol op `f815a092` (migraties + health + smoke groen). - Full staging E2E-suite: **47/47 passed** (4.2m), inclusief blockers-contracten (change-password ≠404, passkey ≠403, subscription checkout ≠500). ## Design-SSoT Frontend volgt `src/client/DESIGN.md` (webshop tokens/patronen); geen ad-hoc styling. records.title: feat(storefront): locale-aware date and time inputs
records.body:
## Summary

- Replace native `type="date"` / `type="datetime-local"` with `DateInput` and `DateTimeInput` so tenants and customers see dates in their store’s format (e.g. dd-mm-jjjj for NL), not the browser’s US default.
- Centralize parsing and display in `src/client/utils/datetime.ts`; API values stay ISO (`YYYY-MM-DD` / `YYYY-MM-DDTHH:mm`).
- Locale resolution: **store country** (`__TENANT__.storeAddressCountry`) first, then shop language (`locale`).

## Test plan

- [ ] NL tenant on staging (`https://demo.staging.pagayo.app`): geboortedatum abonnement, coupon start/eind, rapportage custom periode — invoer toont Europees formaat met browser op en-US
- [ ] Admin: blog/announcement publish datetime, product schedule toolbar
- [ ] Klant: account instellingen geboortedatum, abonnement lid toevoegen
- [ ] `npm run test:client -- src/client/utils/__tests__/datetime.test.ts`
- [ ] Na merge: staging deploy via `workflow_dispatch` (`deploy_mode=staging-only`)

Updates `@aws-sdk/client-s3` from 3.1046.0 to 3.1049.0
Release notes

Sourced from @​aws-sdk/client-s3's releases.

v3.1049.0

3.1049.0(2026-05-18)

Documentation Changes
  • client-evs: Amazon EVS now supports up to 32 hosts per EVS environment, increasing the previous host limit to allow a larger scale of VMware workload deployments and reduce operational overhead. (34718dc5)
New Features
  • clients: update client endpoints as of 2026-05-18 (a5f4e2a2)
  • client-ec2: Amazon VPC IP Address Manager (IPAM) now supports tags on IPAM pool allocations, enabling all standard tagging features for allocations including tag-on-create. (0ac6d448)
  • client-accessanalyzer: Services manage service-linked analyzers through dedicated APIs - CreateServiceLinkedAnalyzer and DeleteServiceLinkedAnalyzer that separate service-linked specific operations from customer-managed operations. It also shows up in ListAnalyzers and GetAnalyzer responses. (fdfcbe80)
  • client-ecs: Amazon ECS now supports Pause lifecycle hooks for service deployments, allowing customers to automatically pause deployments at specified stages and use the new ContinueServiceDeployment API to continue or roll back with confidence. (8437bd6c)
  • client-connect: Amazon Connect Cases now supports SLA durations of up to 2 years (1,051,200 minutes), increased from the previous maximum of 90 days (129,600 minutes). This enables you to track long-running service level agreements for cases that require extended resolution timelines. (045e1382)
  • client-ivs: Adds support for up to 3 mediaTailorPlaybackConfiguration objects in an ad configuration resource (e7a59d85)
  • client-quicksight: Support for dataset enrichment and geo spatial in new data preparation experience (c3036698)
Bug Fixes
  • core/protocols: make error namespace removal unconditional in JSON RPC (#8031) (7cee4f27)
  • client-sts: update imports to new module locations (#8025) (be183b6d)

Updates `wrangler` from 4.91.0 to 4.93.0
Release notes

Sourced from wrangler's releases.

wrangler@4.93.0

Minor Changes

  • #13901 aac7ca0 Thanks @​bghira! - Add wrangler ai models schema command for fetching model schemas

    You can now run wrangler ai models schema <model> to fetch the input and output schema for a Workers AI model from the public model catalog schema endpoint.

  • #12656 ae047ee Thanks @​mikenomitch! - Add --containers-rollout=none

    This allows you to skip deploying a container. This is useful if you know that your container is not going to be updated or you don't have Docker locally, but still want to make changes to your Worker.

  • #13901 aac7ca0 Thanks @​bghira! - Add wrangler ai models list command for querying the Workers AI model catalog

    wrangler ai models list accepts --search, --task, --author, --source, and --hide-experimental, matching the public model catalog search endpoint.

Patch Changes

  • #13948 b25dc0d Thanks @​dependabot! - Update dependencies of "miniflare", "wrangler"

    The following dependency versions have been updated:

    Dependency From To
    workerd 1.20260515.1 1.20260518.1

  • #13882 a4f22bc Thanks @​matingathani! - Throw a clear error when a D1 migration is cancelled instead of silently returning

  • #13950 f78d435 Thanks @​dario-piotrowicz! - Improve the Docker CLI error message to be more actionable.

    Include a link to Docker installation docs, platform-specific instructions for starting the daemon, and guidance for alternative Docker-compatible CLIs.

  • #11896 c5c9e20 Thanks @​staticpayload! - Surface remote proxy session errors

    When remote bindings fail to start, include the controller reason and root cause in the error message to make failures like missing cloudflared clearer.

  • #13932 ebf4b24 Thanks @​zebp! - Fix local Workflow startup when compatibility flags include experimental

    Miniflare now deduplicates compatibility flags for the internal Workflow engine service. This prevents wrangler dev from failing with Compatibility flag specified multiple times: experimental when the user's Worker already enables that flag.

  • #13929 895baf5 Thanks @​Caio-Nogueira! - Prompt to provision a workers.dev subdomain before deploying Workflows

    Wrangler now checks for the account-level workers.dev subdomain when deploying Workflows, even if the Worker is not being published to workers.dev. If the subdomain has not been registered yet, Wrangler prompts to create one before calling the Workflows deploy API so users avoid an opaque server-side deployment failure.

  • #13930 7bcdf45 Thanks @​shiminshen! - Sweep stale .wrangler/tmp/* dirs left behind by abnormal exits

    A wrangler dev session creates .wrangler/tmp/bundle-* and .wrangler/tmp/dev-* directories at startup and removes them via a signal-exit hook on graceful shutdown. When the process exited abnormally (SIGKILL, OOM, host crash) those directories were left behind and accumulated across sessions, slowing down dependency-walking tools that follow the bundle-emitted absolute-path imports.

    wrangler now sweeps entries in .wrangler/tmp/ older than 24 hours when a new temporary directory is requested, bounding the leak regardless of how prior sessions exited.

records.title: deps: Bump lint-staged from 16.4.0 to 17.0.5
records.body:
Bumps [lint-staged](https://github.com/lint-staged/lint-staged) from 16.4.0 to 17.0.5.
Release notes

Sourced from lint-staged's releases.

v17.0.5

Patch Changes

  • #1792 1f67271 - Correctly set the --max-arg-length default value based on the running platform. This controls how very long lists of staged files are split into multiple chunks.

v17.0.4

Patch Changes

  • #1788 f95c1f8 - Another fix for making sure lint-staged adds task modifications correctly to the commit in the following cases:

    • after editing <file> it is staged with git add <file>, and then committed with git commit
    • after editing <file> it is committed with git commit --all without explicit git add
    • after editing <file> it is committed with git commit <pathspec> without explicit git add

    There's new test cases which actually setup the Git pre_commit hook to run lint-staged and verify them. These issues started in v17.0.0 when trying to improve support for committig without having explicitly staged files.

v17.0.3

Patch Changes

  • #1782 06813f9 Thanks @​iiroj! - Fix lint-staged behavior when implicitly committing files without using git add by either:
    • git commit -am "my commit message" where -a (--all) means to automatically stage all tracked modified and deleted files
    • git commit -m "my commit message" . where . is an example of a pathspec where matching files will be staged

v17.0.2

Patch Changes

  • #1779 88670ca Thanks @​iiroj! - Enable immutable GitHub releases

v17.0.1

Patch Changes

  • #1776 4a5664b Thanks @​iiroj! - Adjust GitHub Actions workflow so that automatic publishing works with signed commits.

v17.0.0

Major Changes

  • #1745 e244adf Thanks @​iiroj! - Node.js v20 is no longer supported, and the oldest supported version is now 22.22.1, which is an active LTS version at the time of this release. Node.js 20 will be EOL after April 2026. Please upgrade your Node.js version!

  • #1676 0584e0b Thanks @​outslept! - Lint-staged now tries to verify the installed Git version is at least 2.32.0, released in 2021. If you're using an even older Git version, you need to upgrade it before running lint-staged!

  • #1745 2dcc40a Thanks @​iiroj! - The dependency yaml is now marked as optional and probably won't be installed by default. If you're using a YAML configuration file you should install the package separately:

    npm install --development yaml

    If you're using .lintstagedrc as the config file name (without a file extension), it will be treated as a YAML file. If the content is JSON, consider renaming it to .lintstagedrc.json to avoid needing to install yaml.

Minor Changes

records.title: ci: Bump actions/setup-node from 4 to 6
records.body:
Bumps [actions/setup-node](https://github.com/actions/setup-node) from 4 to 6.
Release notes

Sourced from actions/setup-node's releases.

v6.0.0

What's Changed

Breaking Changes

  • Limit automatic caching to npm, update workflows and documentation by @​priyagupta108 in actions/setup-node#1374

Dependency Upgrades

  • Upgrade ts-jest from 29.1.2 to 29.4.1 and document breaking changes in v5 by @​dependabot[bot] in #1336
  • Upgrade prettier from 2.8.8 to 3.6.2 by @​dependabot[bot] in #1334
  • Upgrade actions/publish-action from 0.3.0 to 0.4.0 by @​dependabot[bot] in #1362

Full Changelog: https://github.com/actions/setup-node/compare/v5...v6.0.0

v5.0.0

What's Changed

Breaking Changes

  • Enhance caching in setup-node with automatic package manager detection by @​priya-kinthali in actions/setup-node#1348

This update, introduces automatic caching when a valid packageManager field is present in your package.json. This aims to improve workflow performance and make dependency management more seamless. To disable this automatic caching, set package-manager-cache: false

steps:
- uses: actions/checkout@v5
- uses: actions/setup-node@v5
  with:
    package-manager-cache: false
  • Upgrade action to use node24 by @​salmanmkc in actions/setup-node#1325

Make sure your runner is on version v2.327.1 or later to ensure compatibility with this release. See Release Notes

Dependency Upgrades

records.title: fix(ci): production deploy_token via GITHUB_EVENT_PATH
records.body:
## Summary
- Production gate steps read `deploy_token` with `jq` from `GITHUB_EVENT_PATH` instead of interpolating into `env`, avoiding empty values when GitHub suppresses inputs that match repository secrets.
- Preflight fails fast on `full` / `production-only` when `deploy_token` is missing from the dispatch payload (clear `gh workflow run` hint).

## Test plan
- [ ] Merge, then `workflow_dispatch` `deploy_mode=full` with `target_ref=main` and vault `deploy_token`; confirm **Migrate D1 (production)** passes **Validate deploy token**.

Made with [Cursor](https://cursor.com)
records.author: Pagayo

records.title: fix(ci): workflow_dispatch deploy_token for production
records.body:
## Summary
`migrate-production` / `deploy-production` used `${{ inputs.deploy_token }}`, which is unset for a top-level `workflow_dispatch`, so `INPUT_TOKEN` was empty and token validation always failed.

Use `${{ github.event.inputs.deploy_token }}` instead.

## Test plan
- [ ] Merge → re-run `deploy_mode=full` with vault `deploy_token`; **Validate deploy token** step must pass.

Made with [Cursor](https://cursor.com)

## Summary
- Adds `POST /api/internal/catalog/kv-full-sync` authenticated with `X-Internal-Secret` (same secret as other internal routes) so ops can run a full catalog KV sync without an admin browser session. Mount is under `/api/internal/catalog` so it is not intercepted by the subscription internal router `use("*")` middleware.
- Extracts shared full-sync logic into `tenant-full-kv-sync.service.ts`; admin `POST /kv-sync/full` delegates to it.
- Woo migration scripts: optional `.dev.vars` / `.env.woo.local` loading, default `CF_ACCOUNT_ID`, `env.woo.example`, and `npm run ops:kv:full:internal` trigger script.

## Test plan

records.title: fix(test): ProductsPage i18n mock stabiliteit (CI OOM)
records.body:
Oplossing voor falende Client Tests (main shard 2) op main: heap OOM / worker crash.

**Oorzaak:** `ProductsPage.test.tsx` mockte `useI18n()` met een nieuwe `t`-functie elke render. Daardoor wijzigde `fetchSelectedProduct` (useCallback met `[t]`) elke render, werd het effect op `selectedProductId` steeds opnieuw getriggerd, en liep de test vast tot geheugen vol was.

**Fix:** `vi.hoisted` met stabiele `t`, `setLocale` en `useI18n`-factory.

Lokaal: `ProductsPage.test.tsx` ~1s; shard 2/8 ~15s.

records.title: Staging batch 20260511 + perf hardening + GPSR fix (schema 2.6.30)
records.body:
## Summary
Verzamelt de staging-batch van 11/12 mei + de afronding van het performance-hardening werkplan (S/M/L/XL) en de fix voor de staging-regressie op product-detail.

### Storefront wijzigingen
- **Plan S/M/L/XL** (performance hardening) reeds gemerged en op productie:
  - **S**: hot-fix age-gate render-blocking; FCP/LCP-budget weer binnen target.
  - **M**: client-side single-flight + event-driven cache voor public settings (24→3 API-calls op staging).
  - **L**: SSR-injectie van `window.__SETTINGS__` + granular staging cache-allowlist + `safeJsonForInlineScript` (XSS-safe).
  - **XL**: granular modulepreload denylist + budget-check (~-49% preloads, ~-84% WebKit warnings).
- **Staging batch 20260511** (`7e31860a..1a46d6ac`): admin members/products, kv-sync, share buttons, i18n, hardened staging E2E.
- **GPSR-fix** (`443abfd8`): bump `@pagayo/schema` → **`^2.6.30`** (GitHub Packages), `products.routes.ts` selecteert `manufacturerName/Address/Contact` weer via typed `p.*` (geen `sql.raw` workaround).

### Schema (parallel)
- `@pagayo/schema@2.6.30` reeds gepubliceerd via Pagayo/pagayo-schema#42 + Pagayo/pagayo-schema#43 (lockfile-sync). Tenant-migratie `20260512120000_product_gpsr_manufacturer.sql` zit in manifest (fan-out-only, idempotente ALTER TABLE).

## Pagayo-pijlers
- **Consistentie**: typed Drizzle-kolommen, manifest-entry volgt zelfde patroon als product_attributes / quantity_stepping.
- **Testbaarheid**: storefront client 1901 tests + worker 3371 tests + volledige staging E2E (41 passed, 3 manual-skip).
- **Foutafhandeling**: product-detail API geeft geen 500 meer; staging eerder rood door `sql.raw('p."manufacturerName"')` (alias-fout + ontbrekende kolommen in 2.6.29) — nu hersteld.
- **SSoT**: GPSR-kolommen + migratie in `@pagayo/schema@2.6.30`; storefront leunt erop.
- **Edge-first**: ongewijzigd; cache/KV-paden blijven leidend.

## Design
Niet van toepassing — geen wijziging aan `src/client/DESIGN.md`. `@pagayo/design` blijft op `^2.4.114` (lockfile parity).

## Verificatie op staging (`demo.staging.pagayo.app`, run `25742439678`)
- Migratie `20260512120000_product_gpsr_manufacturer.sql` succesvol op tenant-D1 + fan-out.
- `GET /api/products/personal-abonnement` → **HTTP 200**.
- E2E:
  - blockers: **3/3 passed**
  - regression: **3/3 passed**
  - full suite: **41 passed, 3 skipped** (~3.4m)

## Test plan
- [x] CI groen op `feature/batch-staging-20260511` (`25742095187`).
- [x] Staging-only deploy + tenant fan-out groen.
- [x] Volledige Playwright staging suite groen.
- [ ] CI op deze PR groen.
- [ ] Squash-merge naar `main` met expliciete Sjoerd-goedkeuring.
- [ ] `deploy_mode=full` met `target_ref=main` (productie via playbook 04, deploy_token in workflow_dispatch).

Made with [Cursor](https://cursor.com)
records.author: Pagayo

records.title: perf(storefront): granular modulepreload (Fase XL)
records.body:
## Summary

Vite's default `build.modulePreload` injecteert `<link rel=\"modulepreload\">` voor de complete transitive dependency-graph van elke dynamic import. Op de storefront homepage levert dat **~49 modulepreload-links** op die de network-queue saturate tijdens FCP/LCP.

Deze PR introduceert een denylist via `modulePreload.resolveDependencies` die non-critical chunks uit de preload-graph haalt: cart-paneel, modals, auth-dropdown, async-data widgets. Browser laadt ze on-demand bij eerste gebruik.

**Verwacht resultaat:** ~49 → ≤15 `<link rel=\"modulepreload\">` op productie.

## Wijzigingen

- **`scripts/modulepreload-denylist.mjs`** (NIEUW, SSoT): denylist + `isPreloadable()` helper, gedeeld tussen `vite.config.ts`, het budget-script en unit tests.
- **`scripts/check-modulepreload-budget.mjs`** (NIEUW): post-build budget guard die het Vite manifest leest en faalt als preloadable-count > BUDGET (20).
- **`vite.config.ts`**: `build.modulePreload.resolveDependencies` filtert deps via `isPreloadable()`.
- **`src/workers/__tests__/modulepreload-denylist.test.ts`** (NIEUW): unit tests voor critical-path chunks (moeten preloaded blijven), gestripte chunks (moeten weg), hash-rotatie stabiliteit en false-positive guards (\"Alert\" vs \"AlertManager\").
- **`eslint.config.mjs`**: `.mjs`/`.cjs` toegevoegd aan files-glob zodat Node globals (`console`, `process`) beschikbaar zijn in `scripts/`.
- **`package.json`**: nieuw npm script \"check:preload-budget\".

## Pagayo-pijlers

1. **Consistentie** — één SSoT voor denylist (`scripts/modulepreload-denylist.mjs`); zowel build-config, post-build script als tests gebruiken dezelfde regex-set.
2. **Testbaarheid** — unit tests voor elke critical chunk + gestripte chunk + hash-rotatie + false-positive guards. Post-build budget assertie als CI-friendly guard.
3. **Foutafhandeling** — script faalt expliciet met exit code 1 + actionable bericht (\"voeg chunk toe aan denylist of refactor\"). Geen stille degradation.
4. **SSoT** — denylist gedeeld via Node ESM import in `.mjs`; geen duplicatie tussen build-config en script/test.
5. **Edge-first** — directe winst voor cold-load: minder modulepreload-links → schonere network-priority queue → snellere FCP/LCP op slow 4G / mobile.

## Werkplan + acceptatie

- Werkplan: [`pagayo-vault/.github/agenda/werkplan-storefront-perf-hardening-20260512.md`](https://github.com/Pagayo/pagayo-storefront/blob/main/../pagayo-vault/.github/agenda/werkplan-storefront-perf-hardening-20260512.md) §Fase XL.
- Acceptance:
  - [x] Build slaagt + budget-script passt op alle 4 entries
  - [x] Unit tests groen (3427 workers + 1904 client)
  - [x] Typecheck + lint groen
  - [ ] Staging: `<link rel=\"modulepreload\">` count ≤ 15
  - [ ] Staging: geen WebKit \"preloaded but not used\" warnings
  - [ ] Staging: LCP onveranderd of beter

## Test plan

- Lokaal: `npm run build:client && npm run check:preload-budget` → PASS budget van 20 op alle 4 entries.
- Staging deploy (`workflow_dispatch deploy_mode=staging-only --ref main -f target_ref=feature/batch-staging-20260512-fase-xl`).
- Playwright verify op `demo.staging.pagayo.app` met Chromium + WebKit:
  - Tel `document.querySelectorAll('link[rel=\"modulepreload\"]').length` na pageload
  - LCP via `PerformanceObserver({ type: 'largest-contentful-paint' })`
  - Console warnings filter op `/preload|prefetch|modulepreload/i`
  - Cart-button click → controleer dat lazy chunk fetch succesvol is en panel opent
- STOP-2 gate: cijfers naar Sjoerd, go/no-go voor productie.
- Productie: `workflow_dispatch deploy_mode=full --ref main -f target_ref=feature/batch-staging-20260512-fase-xl` (zelfde pattern als Plan M/L).

Made with [Cursor](https://cursor.com)
records.author: Pagayo

records.title: perf(storefront): SSR-inject public settings + granular staging cache (Fase L)
records.body:
## Wat

Fase L van het werkplan storefront-perf-hardening. Volgt op Fase M (#152): die consolideerde N+1 client fetches naar één `/api/settings/public` call. Fase L tilt die ene call naar **nul** voor de eerste pageload door:

1. **SSR inject** van de public settings payload in `window.__SETTINGS__`, zodat de client helper bij first read niets hoeft op te halen.
2. **Granular `DISABLE_CACHE` override op staging** — `STAGING_PUBLIC_CACHE=true` laat een strikte allowlist van read-only endpoints (settings/navigation/categories/brands/products/partners/announcements) de version-based Cache API gebruiken, zelfs als `DISABLE_CACHE=true` staat (de status-quo voor admin freshness).

## Hoe

**Backend SSoT.** Nieuwe `loadPublicSettingsPayload(c)` in `src/features/settings/public-settings.service.ts`. Zowel `GET /api/settings/public` als de page-routes SSR-injectie roepen hem aan; één registry+DB-pad, geen duplicatie. Service is fail-silent: registry-leeg of DB-error → `{}` met warn-log, nooit een throw die de pagina-render breekt.

**SSR injectie.** Page-routes hebben al een post-render middleware die `__CATEGORIES__`, `__NAVIGATION__` en `ageGateEnabled` in `</body>` injecteert. Aan diezelfde `Promise.all` is `loadPublicSettingsPayload(c)` toegevoegd en het resulterende script wordt erbij gerendered met een nieuwe `safeJsonForInlineScript` helper (escape `<` → `\u003c`, plus U+2028/U+2029).

**Client.** `getPublicSettings()` heeft een nieuwe eerste stap: `consumeSsrInjectedSettings()` leest `window.__SETTINGS__`, hydrateert de storefrontCache en verwijdert de global, zodat invalidation-events alsnog een refetch triggeren. Bij ontbrekend/niet-object payload: oude flow (storefrontCache → in-flight → fetch) intact.

**Cache laag.** Nieuwe `isStagingPublicCacheAllowed(pathname)` in `response-cache.ts` met allowlist van 8 paden. `worker.ts` cache-middleware:

\`\`\`ts
const stagingAllowsCache =
  c.env.DISABLE_CACHE === \"true\" &&
  c.env.STAGING_PUBLIC_CACHE === \"true\" &&
  isCacheable(method, path) &&
  isStagingPublicCacheAllowed(path);
\`\`\`

Productie ongewijzigd (`DISABLE_CACHE=\"false\"` ⇒ allowlist niet geraadpleegd, normale cache-flow). Local dev: \`STAGING_PUBLIC_CACHE\` niet gezet ⇒ huidig bypass-gedrag.

## Pagayo-pijlers

1. **Consistentie** — server- en client-pad voor public settings delen één service + één wire format. `getPublicSettings()` is nu de enige client-entry, met identiek fallback-pad voor SSR-miss en netwerk-error.
2. **Testbaarheid** — 3 nieuwe test files (service unit, SSR-script XSS-veilig, response-cache allowlist) + uitbreiding van bestaande client tests met SSR-injection scenario's. Geen `.skip`.
3. **Foutafhandeling** — service fail-silent (`{}`), middleware fail-silent (`null` + console.warn), client fallback intact. Geen pagina-breekpunt bij DB cold start.
4. **SSoT** — `loadPublicSettingsPayload` is single source voor zowel HTTP envelope als HTML-inject; registry-keys blijven SSoT voor publieke shape.
5. **Edge-first** — read-only public endpoints kunnen nu op staging Cache API HIT'en. Mutaties triggeren `bumpResponseCacheVersion`; eerstvolgende read wordt MISS en herlaadt.

## Verwachte impact

- **Cold staging** `demo.staging.pagayo.app`: 0 settings calls bij eerste pageload (`window.__SETTINGS__` hydrate). Eindstand inclusief de SSR uitvoer-tijd.
- **Warm staging**: tweede pageload `/api/settings/public` HIT met `x-response-cache: HIT` header (allowlist actief).
- **Productie zwembad**: geen functionele wijziging in cache-gedrag (staging-only var), wel SSR-inject ⇒ 1 minder client-fetch per cold pageload.

## Test plan

- [ ] Wacht op CI groen (stack-check, validate, workers tests, client tests x8 shards, admin critical gate)
- [ ] `gh workflow run deploy-cloudflare.yml --ref main -f deploy_mode=staging-only -f target_ref=feature/batch-staging-20260512-fase-l`
- [ ] Playwright verify `demo.staging.pagayo.app` (cold + warm) — meet settings calls + cache headers, capture screenshot
- [ ] Vraag go van Sjoerd
- [ ] Productie via `--ref main -f deploy_mode=full -f target_ref=…` (zie Plan M lessons learned)
- [ ] Playwright verify `www.zwembadhetelderink.nl` productie

## Werkplan

Zie [`pagayo-vault/.github/agenda/werkplan-storefront-perf-hardening-20260512.md`](../pagayo-vault/.github/agenda/werkplan-storefront-perf-hardening-20260512.md) §Fase L.

Made with [Cursor](https://cursor.com)
records.author: Pagayo

records.title: perf(storefront): consolidate public settings client fetches (Fase M)
records.body:
## Samenvatting

Fase M van het storefront perf-hardening plan: tien onafhankelijke `/api/settings/public` consumers en acht footer-specifieke `/api/settings/<key>` fetches worden samengevoegd tot één gedeelde `getPublicSettings()` helper met single-flight + event-driven cache.

Volgt op Plan S (#151, age-gate hot-fix). Werkplan: [`pagayo-vault/.github/agenda/werkplan-storefront-perf-hardening-20260512.md`](https://github.com/Pagayo/pagayo-vault/blob/main/.github/agenda/werkplan-storefront-perf-hardening-20260512.md).

## Architectuur

Nieuwe helper `src/client/utils/public-settings.ts`:
- In-memory promise dedup (single-flight): concurrent callers delen één fetch
- Hergebruikt bestaand `storefrontCache` (`getValidatedCached` + `setCache`) voor event-driven invalidation; geen TTL
- Subscribet op `subscribeStorefrontCacheInvalidation`: cache + inflight worden gewist bij admin save zodat volgende read fresh is
- Fail-silent: gooit nooit, retourneert `{}` zodat consumer-defaults blijven werken

Backend ongewijzigd: alle 8 footer-keys waren al `public: true` in `settings-registry`, dus `/api/settings/public` retourneerde ze al — `Footer.tsx` leest ze nu gewoon uit één bulk-payload in plaats van 8 separate calls.

## Wijzigingen

| Bestand | Aard |
|---|---|
| `src/client/utils/public-settings.ts` *(nieuw)* | Single-flight helper + types |
| `src/client/utils/__tests__/public-settings.test.ts` *(nieuw)* | 7 tests: dedup, cache, invalidation, error fallback |
| `src/client/utils/age-gate-public.ts` | gebruik `getPublicSettings()` |
| `src/client/utils/vat-settings.ts` | eigen cache-key vervalt |
| `src/client/utils/stock-badge.ts` | eigen cache-key vervalt |
| `src/client/lib/analytics.ts` | gebruik `getPublicSettings()` |
| `src/client/features/checkout/CheckoutPage.tsx` | `cache: 'no-store'` weg |
| `src/client/features/auth/AuthPage.tsx` | `cache: 'no-store'` weg |
| `src/client/features/account/OrderDetailPage.tsx` | gebruik `getPublicSettings()` |
| `src/client/features/checkout/PaymentReturn.tsx` | gebruik `getPublicSettings()` |
| `src/client/pages/home.tsx` | gebruik `getPublicSettings()` |
| `src/client/features/layout/Footer.tsx` | 8 fetches → 1 |
| `src/client/__tests__/features/layout/Footer.test.tsx` | mocks naar `/api/settings/public` payload-shape |
| `src/client/features/checkout/__tests__/CheckoutPage.subscriptionAccess.test.tsx` | assert geen `cache: no-store` meer |

Net: 14 files, +413 / -352 (≈ -240 SLOC duplicate fetch logic geschrapt).

## Verwacht effect per pageload

| Tenant | Voor (post-Plan-S baseline) | Na | Δ |
|---|---|---|---|
| Staging (`demo.staging.pagayo.app`, age-gate AAN) | 16× `/api/settings/public` + 8× footer-keys = **24** | **1×** `/api/settings/public` | **−23 settings calls** |
| Productie (`zwembadhetelderink.nl`, age-gate UIT) | 4× `/api/settings/public` + 8× footer-keys = **12** | **1×** | **−11 settings calls** |

Niet in scope: `homepageConfig`, `opening-hours`, `passConfig` — die zijn aparte endpoints, niet `/api/settings/public`.

## Test plan

- [x] `npm run typecheck` (workers) groen
- [x] `npm run lint` groen
- [x] `npm test` groen (3361 tests)
- [x] `npm run test:client` groen (1901 tests, inclusief 7 nieuwe + 2 ge-update consumer tests)
- [ ] CI groen
- [ ] Staging deploy via `workflow_dispatch` met `deploy_mode=staging-only`
- [ ] Playwright verify op staging: `/api/settings/public` daalt van 16× naar 1× per pageload, age-gate modal werkt nog
- [ ] Playwright verify op productie: 3 footer chunks weg (waren al weg post-Plan-S), `/api/settings/public` daalt naar 1×

## Risico's

| Risico | Mitigatie |
|---|---|
| Race-condition tussen tabs: admin save terwijl andere tab een inflight fetch heeft | `subscribeStorefrontCacheInvalidation` callback zet `inflight = null` + wist cache; volgende read na save is altijd vers |
| Helper retourneert `{}` bij fail → consumers krijgen lege string voor sommige keys | Elke consumer leest met `?? defaultValue` (behouden uit oorspronkelijke implementatie) |
| Cache stale na deploy door Cloudflare Cache API | Response cache versie bump bij settings mutatie (`bumpResponseCacheVersion` bestaande mechaniek), unchanged |
| Pre-bestaande typecheck:client warnings/errors in onafhankelijke files | Buiten Plan M scope; CI runt alleen workers typecheck |

## Refs

- Plan S (voorganger): #151
- Werkplan B: [`werkplan-storefront-perf-hardening-20260512.md`](https://github.com/Pagayo/pagayo-vault/blob/main/.github/agenda/werkplan-storefront-perf-hardening-20260512.md)

Made with [Cursor](https://cursor.com)
records.author: Pagayo

records.title: fix(storefront): age-gate mount-guard + no-store removal
records.body:
## Samenvatting

Hot-fix voor regressie in release `0e7b4a74` (batch staging 2026-05-11, age-gate intro). Voor tenants zonder age-gate werd de hele feature toch geladen — `AgeGateModal` mountte onvoorwaardelijk, 3 chunks werden gepreload, en een `cache: 'no-store'` fetch naar `/api/settings/public` ging bij elke pageload over de cache heen.

## Wijzigingen (3 bestanden, 22 insertions, 8 deletions)

### 1. `src/features/content/page.routes.ts`
Post-render middleware fetcht nu naast categories + navigation ook `ageGate.enabled` van tenant D1 en injecteert `window.__TENANT__.ageGateEnabled` als derde script-block. Volgt bestaand pattern voor `__CATEGORIES__` / `__NAVIGATION__`. Faal-tolerant: bij D1-fout default `false`.

### 2. `src/client/main.tsx`
`AgeGateModal` mount nu alleen als `window.__TENANT__.ageGateEnabled === true`. Tenants met gate UIT preloaden de 3 age-gate chunks niet meer.

### 3. `src/client/utils/age-gate-public.ts`
`cache: 'no-store'` verwijderd uit `fetchAgeGateFromNetwork`. `subscribeStorefrontCacheInvalidation` verzorgt al cross-tab refresh bij admin toggle; HTTP cache mag deze response normaal cachen.

## Effect

| Tenant | Voor | Na |
|---|---|---|
| age-gate UIT (productie: zwembadhetelderink.nl) | 3 chunks gepreload, 1 no-store fetch/pageload | 0 chunks gepreload, 0 no-store fetch |
| age-gate AAN (staging: demo.staging.pagayo.app) | normale flow met no-store overhead | normale flow, response mag cachen |

Geen schema- of type-wijzigingen. Geen design-systeem impact.

## Pagayo-pijlers

1. **Consistentie** — zelfde injection-pattern als `__CATEGORIES__` / `__NAVIGATION__`
2. **Testbaarheid** — `npm test` (workers, 3361 pass) + `npm run test:client` (1894 pass) groen; bestaande `age-gate-suppressed-path.test.ts` ongewijzigd
3. **Foutafhandeling** — `getAgeGateSettings` faalt fail-silent, default `enabled: false`; geen UI-breekpunt
4. **SSoT** — één bron voor age-gate state (tenant D1 setting → window.__TENANT__); geen duplicate inline check
5. **Edge-first** — door no-store verwijdering kan Cloudflare Cache API (productie) zijn werk doen

## Test plan

- [x] `npm run typecheck` (workers)
- [x] `npm run lint`
- [x] `npm test` (workers vitest)
- [x] `npm run test:client` (client vitest, 215 files, 1894 tests)
- [ ] CI groen op deze SHA
- [ ] Staging deploy via `workflow_dispatch` met `deploy_mode=staging-only`
- [ ] Playwright verify op https://demo.staging.pagayo.app (age-gate AAN: modal verschijnt nog steeds)
- [ ] Playwright verify op productie tenant zonder age-gate (3 chunks weg, 1 no-store call weg)

## Refs

- Werkplan A: `pagayo-vault/.github/agenda/werkplan-storefront-age-gate-hotfix-20260512.md`
- Werkplan B (vervolg-fasen): `pagayo-vault/.github/agenda/werkplan-storefront-perf-hardening-20260512.md`
- Regressie geintroduceerd in: commit 0e7b4a74 (#149)

Made with [Cursor](https://cursor.com)

records.title: fix: quote table names in tenant migration PRAGMA check
records.body:
## Context
Production full deploy (run 25675470878) failed in **Pre-deploy tenant fan-out migrations**: D1 REST returned SQLITE_ERROR on `PRAGMA table_info(order)` — `order` is a reserved keyword without quotes.

## Change
- Escape table name and use `PRAGMA table_info("...")` (same pattern as `tenant-schema-guard.ts`).
- Update unit test mock to match quoted SQL.

## Verification
- `npx vitest run -c vitest.workers.config.ts src/features/tenant-migrations/__tests__/tenant-migrations.service.test.ts` — 20 passed.

Re-run playbook **04** (`deploy_mode=full`, `target_ref=main`).

records.title: release: batch staging 2026-05-11 → main (POS cadence, finance, check-in, design 2.4.112)
records.body:
## Samenvatting

Merge van **`feature/batch-staging-20260510`** naar `main` voor productie-release. Staging is eerder gevalideerd op **https://demo.staging.pagayo.app** (Workers deploy vanaf deze branch).

Storefront-tip na merge: **`fcdbf129`** (controleer na squash-merge de uiteindelijke `main`-SHA in GitHub).

## Consumer-packages (registry-pariteit)

- **`@pagayo/config@^1.15.0`** — gepubliceerd vanaf `pagayo-config` feature-branch; o.a. treasurer/workbench defaults (`posCloseCadence`, enz.).
- **`@pagayo/design@^2.4.112`** — tag **`v2.4.112`** gepubliceerd; storefront lockfile + **`PAGAYO_DESIGN_SOURCE=node_modules npm run copy-design`** + `src/workers/generated/design-asset-version.ts` staan in lijn (CI: `npm run design:verify-asset-version`).

## Bevindingen om mee te nemen (post-merge / Dependabot)

1. **`@pagayo/design` lockfile-bump zonder `copy-design`** — Dependabot-run [CI #25660657658](https://github.com/Pagayo/pagayo-storefront/actions/runs/25660657658) faalde op `design:verify-asset-version` (`DESIGN_ASSET_VERSION` nog `2.4.111` terwijl lockfile `2.4.112` had). **Fix:** altijd `PAGAYO_DESIGN_SOURCE=node_modules npm run copy-design` + commit `design-asset-version.ts` (en eventuele `public/design/**`-wijzigingen) in dezelfde PR als de design-bump. Zie release-playbooks README (*Lokale pagayo-design/dist vs registry*).

2. **Dependabot PR [#146](https://github.com/Pagayo/pagayo-storefront/pull/146)** — titel *“1 directory met 23 updates”* = repo-root (`/`), **23 semver-bumps** in de `production-dependencies`-groep; git-diff is vooral **`package.json` + `package-lock.json`**. Inhoud overlapt grotendeels met wat al op deze branch zit (`@pagayo/config`/`@pagayo/design`, hono, wrangler, tiptap, zod, …). **Na merge van deze PR:** #146 sluiten als *superseded* (of rebasen en laten vervallen) tenzij je bewust nog losse bumps wilt behouden.

3. **`pagayo-config` publish op Linux** — eerste publish faalde op `npm ci` door ontbrekende `@emnapi/core`/`@emnapi/runtime` in lockfile (peer van o.a. `@napi-rs/wasm-runtime`). Opgelost door **expliciete devDependencies** `@emnapi/core` en `@emnapi/runtime` in `pagayo-config` + lockfile-sync. Houd dit patroon aan voor vergelijkbare CI-fouten.

4. **Design bundle-grootte** — `pagayo-design` build-validation: admin-CSS net boven oude 600KB-grens; plafond test naar **650KB** voor stabiele CI.

## Playbook-referenties

- **03 — Main-pariteit:** deze merge brengt staging-werk naar `main`.
- **04 — Productie:** na groene `main`-CI volgt handmatige `workflow_dispatch` met `deploy_mode=full` (of `production-only`) + `deploy_token` + `target_ref` conform `deploy-cloudflare.yml`.

## Checklist na merge

- [ ] CI op `main` voor merge-SHA groen
- [ ] Productie-deploy volgens vault playbook **04** (geen token in issues/chat)
- [ ] Optioneel: `pagayo-maintenance` `npm run test:smoke:headers` tegen productie-URL indien van toepassing

## Samenvatting

- Client Vitest: polyfill `requestAnimationFrame` / `cancelAnimationFrame` in setup.
- Verwante batch-wijzigingen die op dezelfde branch stonden (o.a. check-in / verify display).

## Deploy

records.title: fix(pos): admin CSS base before tenant pos bundle
records.body:
## Probleem
POS-serverlayout laadde eerst `${theme}/pos.css` en daarna `fresh/admin.css`. De laatste stylesheet wint bij gelijke specificiteit, waardoor **admin-webshop-styling** POS-layout en tokens kon **overschrijven** — vooral zichtbaar op productie (zelfde bundles, verkeerde volgorde).

## Oplossing
- Eerst `fresh/admin.css` (gedeelde basis), daarna `/pos.css` zodat POS-laag bovenop blijft.
- `public/pos.html`: `revolutionary` → `fresh` voor consistentie met SSR en `public/assets/pos.html`.

## Deploy
Na merge: zoals gebruikelijk staging checken, daarna productie-deploy indien akkoord.

records.title: ci(design): git-tag parity using PAGAYO_DESIGN_READ_PAT
records.body:
## Vereist vóór groene CI
Repository secret **`PAGAYO_DESIGN_READ_PAT`** (nieuwe PAT met read op `Pagayo/pagayo-design` — **revoke** elke per ongeluk gelekte token eerst).

Zet secret via GitHub UI of lokaal:
```bash
gh secret set PAGAYO_DESIGN_READ_PAT --repo Pagayo/pagayo-storefront
```
(dan token plakken wanneer gevraagd — niet in issues/chat)

## Inhoud
- `verify-design-npm-matches-git-tag.sh`: gebruikt `PAGAYO_DESIGN_READ_TOKEN` env; in Actions verplicht als `GITHUB_ACTIONS=true`.
- CI + deploy **Validate** job: `corepack enable` + parity stap met secret mapping.

records.title: fix(ci): maak staging tenant runtime canaries opt-in
records.body:
## Context

Type wijziging: fix

Scope: deploy workflow + tenant runtime monitor + canary script

Issue/werkplan: run 24982120069 faalde op hardcoded staging slug demo die niet in PLATFORM_DB bestaat.

## Kernfix

- Verwijdert hardcoded demo defaults voor staging canaries.
- Leest staging canaries uit optionele repo vars:
  - STAGING_TENANT_RUNTIME_CANARY_SLUGS
  - STAGING_TENANT_RUNTIME_CANARY_HOSTS
  - STAGING_TENANT_CUSTOM_DOMAIN_CANARY_HOSTS
- Laat verify-tenant-runtime-canaries.ts netjes slagen met skip-log als geen canaries geconfigureerd zijn.

## Verificatiebewijs

- No-config scenario: script logt skip en exit 0.
- Foutpad met ongeldige slug blijft failen (exit != 0).

## Risico en Rollback

Risico: laag. Alleen canary-gating gedrag voor staging zonder geconfigureerde tenants.

Rollback: revert deze commit en herstel hardcoded staging defaults.

records.title: deps: bump jsdom from 28.1.0 to 29.1.0
records.body:
[//]: # (dependabot-start) ⚠️ **Dependabot is rebasing this PR** ⚠️ Rebasing might not happen immediately, so don't worry if this takes some time. Note: if you make any changes to this PR yourself, they will take precedence over the rebase. --- [//]: # (dependabot-end) Bumps [jsdom](https://github.com/jsdom/jsdom) from 28.1.0 to 29.1.0.
Release notes

Sourced from jsdom's releases.

v29.1.0

  • Added basic support for the ratio CSS type. (@​asamuzaK)
  • Fixed getComputedStyle() sometimes returning outdated results after CSS was modified. (@​asamuzaK)

v29.0.2

  • Significantly improved and sped up getComputedStyle(). Computed value rules are now applied across a broader set of properties, and include fixes related to inheritance, defaulting keywords, custom properties, and color-related values such as currentcolor and system colors. (@​asamuzaK)
  • Fixed CSS 'background' and 'border' shorthand parsing. (@​asamuzaK)

v29.0.1

  • Fixed CSS parsing of 'border', 'background', and their sub-shorthands containing keywords or var(). (@​asamuzaK)
  • Fixed getComputedStyle() to return a more functional CSSStyleDeclaration object, including indexed access support, which regressed in v29.0.0.

v29.0.0

Breaking changes:

  • Node.js v22.13.0+ is now the minimum supported v22 version (was v22.12.0+).

Other changes:

  • Overhauled the CSSOM implementation, replacing the @acemir/cssom and cssstyle dependencies with fresh internal implementations built on webidl2js wrappers and the css-tree parser. Serialization, parsing, and API behavior is improved in various ways, especially around edge cases.
  • Added CSSCounterStyleRule and CSSNamespaceRule to jsdom Windows.
  • Added cssMediaRule.matches and cssSupportsRule.matches getters.
  • Added proper media query parsing in MediaList, using css-tree instead of naive comma-splitting. Invalid queries become "not all" per spec.
  • Added cssKeyframeRule.keyText getter/setter validation.
  • Added cssStyleRule.selectorText setter validation: invalid selectors are now rejected.
  • Added styleSheet.ownerNode, styleSheet.href, and styleSheet.title.
  • Added bad port blocking per the fetch specification, preventing fetches to commonly-abused ports.
  • Improved Document initialization performance by lazily initializing the CSS selector engine, avoiding ~0.5 ms of overhead per Document. (@​thypon)
  • Fixed a memory leak when stylesheets were removed from the document.
  • Fixed CSSStyleDeclaration modifications to properly trigger custom element reactions.
  • Fixed nested @media rule parsing.
  • Fixed CSSStyleSheet's "disallow modification" flag not being checked in all mutation methods.
  • Fixed XMLHttpRequest's response getter returning parsed JSON during the LOADING state instead of null.
  • Fixed getComputedStyle() crashing in XHTML documents when stylesheets contained at-rules such as @page or @font-face.
  • Fixed a potential hang in synchronous XMLHttpRequest caused by a race condition with the worker thread's idle timeout.
Commits

records.title: deps: bump vite-plugin-static-copy from 3.2.0 to 4.1.0
records.body:
Bumps [vite-plugin-static-copy](https://github.com/sapphi-red/vite-plugin-static-copy) from 3.2.0 to 4.1.0.
Release notes

Sourced from vite-plugin-static-copy's releases.

vite-plugin-static-copy@4.1.0

Minor Changes

  • #251 7672842 Thanks @​sapphi-red! - Add name property to the rename object form and allow rename functions to return a RenameObject. The name property replaces the file's basename (filename + extension), and can be combined with stripBase to both flatten directory structure and rename the file in one step. Rename functions can now return { name, stripBase } objects instead of only strings, making it easier to declaratively control output paths from dynamic rename logic.

    // node_modules/lib/dist/index.js → vendor/lib.js
{ src: 'node_modules/lib/dist/index.js', dest: 'vendor', rename: { name: 'lib.js', stripBase: true } }

    // src/pages/events/test.html → dist/events/index.html
    
{ src: 'src/pages/**/*.html', dest: 'dist/', rename: { stripBase: 2, name: 'index.html' } }

vite-plugin-static-copy@4.0.1

Patch Changes

  • #249 c6bf44c Thanks @​sapphi-red! - Fix absolute dest paths being nested under the output directory

    When dest was an absolute path and the source file had a directory component (structured output), the path was incorrectly converted to a relative path, causing files to be nested under the build output directory instead of being copied to the specified absolute path.

    { src: 'foo/foo.txt', dest: '/home/user/my-repo/bar' }

    Before: /home/user/my-repo/dist/home/user/my-repo/bar/foo/foo.txt After: /home/user/my-repo/bar/foo/foo.txt

  • #247 d3af79e Thanks @​sapphi-red! - Fix rename.stripBase to work correctly with ../ paths

    Previously, stripBase counted .. as directory segments, causing incorrect output paths when copying from parent directories.

    { src: '../../src/pages/**/*.html', dest: 'dist/', rename: { stripBase: 2 } }

    Before: dist/src/pages/events/test.html After: dist/events/test.html

    { src: '../../src/pages/**/*.html', dest: 'dist/', rename: { stripBase: true } }

    Before: dist/src/pages/events/test.html After: dist/test.html

vite-plugin-static-copy@4.0.0

Major Changes

  • #235 b2edc86 Thanks @​sapphi-red! - Simplify glob behavior and always preserve directory structure.

records.title: Feature/batch staging 20260426
records.body:
## Context

`Type wijziging:` `<feature|fix|chore|refactor|infra|docs>`

`Scope:` `<welke onderdelen raken we>`

`Issue/werkplan:` `<link of n.v.t.>`

## Blocking Checklist

- [ ] Ik werk vanuit een branch (niet direct op `main`)
- [ ] Wijziging volgt bestaand pattern in deze repo
- [ ] Nieuwe/gewijzigde paden hebben tests waar nodig
- [ ] `npm run lint` is groen
- [ ] `npm run typecheck` is groen
- [ ] `npm run test` is groen
- [ ] Geen `.skip`/`xit` toegevoegd
- [ ] Errors blijven gestructureerd (`code`, `message`, `details`)
- [ ] Geen secrets toegevoegd in code, logs of config
- [ ] Ik heb impact op `pagayo-maintenance`-smokes beoordeeld en bijgewerkt waar nodig

## Staging/Production Gate

- [ ] Merge naar `main` is bedoeld voor staging-validatie
- [ ] Productie gebeurt alleen via `workflow_dispatch` met `deploy_mode=full|production-only`
- [ ] Voor productie is `target_ref` expliciet
- [ ] Voor productie is `deploy_token` vereist
- [ ] `preprod-guard` moet slagen tegen `releases/current.json`

## Verificatiebewijs

Plak hier de kernoutput (kort):

```text
lint:
typecheck:
tests:
smoke (indien van toepassing):
```

## Risico en Rollback

`Risico:` `<laag|middel|hoog> + korte toelichting`

`Rollback:` `<concrete stappen>`

## Notitie voor eenpitter-flow

Human approval is niet de primaire gate. De technische gates in CI en deze checklist zijn leidend voor merge/deploy.

  • 912a49b: Fix HTML character escaping in markdown roundtrip. HTML entities (<, >, &, ") are now decoded to literal characters when parsing markdown into the editor. <, >, and & are re-encoded when serializing back to markdown, while " is preserved as a literal character since double quotes are ordinary in markdown. Code detection for skipping encoding now uses the code: true extension spec instead of hardcoded type names. Literal characters inside code blocks and inline code are always preserved.

Updates `@playwright/test` from 1.58.2 to 1.59.1
Release notes

Sourced from @​playwright/test's releases.

v1.59.1

Bug Fixes

  • [Windows] Reverted hiding console window when spawning browser processes, which caused regressions including broken codegen, --ui and show commands (#39990)

v1.59.0

🎬 Screencast

New page.screencast API provides a unified interface for capturing page content with:

  • Screencast recordings
  • Action annotations
  • Visual overlays
  • Real-time frame capture
  • Agentic video receipts

Screencast recording — record video with precise start/stop control, as an alternative to the recordVideo option:

await page.screencast.start({ path: 'video.webm' });
// ... perform actions ...
await page.screencast.stop();

Action annotations — enable built-in visual annotations that highlight interacted elements and display action titles during recording:

await page.screencast.showActions({ position: 'top-right' });

screencast.showActions() accepts position ('top-left', 'top', 'top-right', 'bottom-left', 'bottom', 'bottom-right'), duration (ms per annotation), and fontSize (px). Returns a disposable to stop showing actions.

Action annotations can also be enabled in test fixtures via the video option:

// playwright.config.ts
export default defineConfig({
  use: {
    video: {
      mode: 'on',
      show: {
        actions: { position: 'top-left' },
        test: { position: 'top-right' },
      },
</tr></table>

Updates `wrangler` from 4.80.0 to 4.85.0
Release notes

Sourced from wrangler's releases.

wrangler@4.84.1

Patch Changes

  • #13615 8fec8b8 Thanks @​dependabot! - Update dependencies of "miniflare", "wrangler"

    The following dependency versions have been updated:

    Dependency From To
    workerd 1.20260420.1 1.20260421.1

  • #13572 a610749 Thanks @​dario-piotrowicz! - Fix wrangler types --check ignoring --env-interface and secondary --config entries

    Previously, wrangler types --check ran its staleness check before resolving the --env-interface flag and before collecting secondary worker entry points from additional --config arguments. This meant it could incorrectly report types as up to date when they were actually stale due to a different env interface name or changes in secondary worker configs. The check now runs after all options are fully resolved, so it correctly detects mismatches.

  • Updated dependencies [8fec8b8, 2f3d7b9]:

    • miniflare@4.20260421.0

wrangler@4.84.0

Minor Changes

  • #13326 4a9ba90 Thanks @​mattzcarey! - Add Artifacts binding support to wrangler

    You can now configure Artifacts bindings in your wrangler configuration:

    // wrangler.jsonc
{
  "artifacts": [{ "binding": "MY_ARTIFACTS", "namespace": "default" }]
}

    Type generation produces the correct Artifacts type reference from the workerd type definitions:

    interface Env {
  MY_ARTIFACTS: Artifacts;
}

  • #13567 d8c895a Thanks @​gpanders! - Rename the documented containers SSH config option to ssh

    Wrangler now accepts and documents containers.ssh in config files while continuing to accept containers.wrangler_ssh as an undocumented backwards-compatible alias. Wrangler still sends and reads wrangler_ssh when talking to the containers API.

  • #13571 7dc0433 Thanks @​must108! - Add regional and jurisdictional placement constraints for Containers. Users can now set constraints.regions and constraints.jurisdiction in wrangler config to control where containers run.

  • #12600 50bf819 Thanks @​penalosa! - Use workerd's debug port to power cross-process service bindings, Durable Objects, and tail workers via the dev registry. This enables Durable Object RPC via the dev registry, and is an overall stability improvement.

Patch Changes

records.title: Feature/batch staging 20260426
records.body:
## Context

`Type wijziging:` `<feature|fix|chore|refactor|infra|docs>`

`Scope:` `<welke onderdelen raken we>`

`Issue/werkplan:` `<link of n.v.t.>`

## Blocking Checklist

- [ ] Ik werk vanuit een branch (niet direct op `main`)
- [ ] Wijziging volgt bestaand pattern in deze repo
- [ ] Nieuwe/gewijzigde paden hebben tests waar nodig
- [ ] `npm run lint` is groen
- [ ] `npm run typecheck` is groen
- [ ] `npm run test` is groen
- [ ] Geen `.skip`/`xit` toegevoegd
- [ ] Errors blijven gestructureerd (`code`, `message`, `details`)
- [ ] Geen secrets toegevoegd in code, logs of config
- [ ] Ik heb impact op `pagayo-maintenance`-smokes beoordeeld en bijgewerkt waar nodig

## Staging/Production Gate

- [ ] Merge naar `main` is bedoeld voor staging-validatie
- [ ] Productie gebeurt alleen via `workflow_dispatch` met `deploy_mode=full|production-only`
- [ ] Voor productie is `target_ref` expliciet
- [ ] Voor productie is `deploy_token` vereist
- [ ] `preprod-guard` moet slagen tegen `releases/current.json`

## Verificatiebewijs

Plak hier de kernoutput (kort):

```text
lint:
typecheck:
tests:
smoke (indien van toepassing):
```

## Risico en Rollback

`Risico:` `<laag|middel|hoog> + korte toelichting`

`Rollback:` `<concrete stappen>`

## Notitie voor eenpitter-flow

Human approval is niet de primaire gate. De technische gates in CI en deze checklist zijn leidend voor merge/deploy.

<!-- CURSOR_SUMMARY -->
---

> [!NOTE]
> **High Risk**
> Updates core admin order mutation logic (status/paymentStatus + payment row/event writes) and modifies payment webhook side effects (subscription activation) plus contact email transport fallbacks, which can affect order state, notifications, and fulfillment flows if incorrect.
> 
> **Overview**
> Adds **inline admin status/payment editing** for orders with debounced autosave, expanded status/payment enums + i18n, and backend support via stricter `PUT /api/admin/orders/:orderId` validation, cross-field transition rules, and syncing `order_payment` + `order_event` updates.
> 
> Refactors **Roles** and **Staff** into master-detail workspace flows with inline create modes, toolbar actions, selection handling, and (for roles) autosave PATCH updates; router entries for `/admin/roles/new` and `/admin/staff/create` now render the workspace pages instead of dedicated create pages.
> 
> Hardens **contact notification delivery**: safe handling when `executionCtx.waitUntil` isn’t available, clearer transport selection, and new fallbacks using `API_STACK_HTTP` and/or internal secrets; adds/updates tests and a new controlled e2e contact submit spec. Also ensures subscription activation runs on paid Stripe/Mollie events (even if already paid) with updated tests.
> 
> Includes small infra/docs/script tweaks: adds reusable `deployer-preflight` to the Cloudflare deploy workflow, clarifies deployment docs, prefers local `pagayo-design` build when present, and extends a backfill script with `sendWelcomeEmails`.
> 
> <sup>Reviewed by [Cursor Bugbot](https://cursor.com/bugbot) for commit 22b78703c1e73b419525b58ae11e256662afd27f. Configure [here](https://www.cursor.com/dashboard/bugbot).</sup>
<!-- /CURSOR_SUMMARY -->

records.title: fix(ci): repair corrupt deploy-cloudflare.yml
records.body:
## Context

`deploy-cloudflare.yml` op main was YAML-invalid — GitHub Actions API weigerde `workflow_dispatch` met:

```
HTTP 422: failed to parse workflow: (Line: 1310, Col: 5): 'if' is already defined
```

Gevolg: elke push naar main triggerde een zero-duration "phantom failure" omdat de workflow niet parse-baar was.

## Root cause

In de `migrate-production` job is de bash-body van de `Verify schema alignment (production)` step corrupt geraakt tijdens een merge. Fragmenten van een `deploy-production` `needs: [...]` / `if: >-` block landden MIDDEN in de bash:

```yaml
if [ ! -f "$SCHEMA_FILE" ]; then
  echo "⚠️ No expected-schema.json found — skipping verification", preprod-guard]
if: >-
  ${{
    !cancelled() &&
    ...
    needs.preprod-guard ".\"$TABLE\".columns[]" "$SCHEMA_FILE" | sort)
```

De YAML-parser zag hierdoor een tweede `if:` key op job-niveau → parse error.

## Fix

Bash-body van de step hersteld naar het patroon van de `Verify schema alignment (staging)` step (regel 467+), met env `production`:

- `exit 0` / `fi` terug toegevoegd na de skip-echo
- `echo "🔍 Verifying schema alignment for Platform D1 (production)..."` + `ERRORS=0`
- `for TABLE in ...` loop met `EXPECTED_COLS=$(jq -r ...)`
- Ontbrekende `--env production` flag toegevoegd aan de `wrangler d1 execute` call

**Geen andere wijzigingen.** De bestaande `deploy-production` job (regel 1366+) was intact en is niet aangeraakt — preprod-guard wiring volgt later in een separate PR indien gewenst.

## Verificatie

- `python3 -c 'import yaml; yaml.safe_load(...)'` → OK
- `actionlint` → alleen pre-existing shellcheck info/warnings, geen nieuwe issues, geen YAML errors
- Diff: 10 insertions, 11 deletions (1 bestand)

records.title: deps: bump dompurify from 3.3.3 to 3.4.1
records.body:
Bumps [dompurify](https://github.com/cure53/DOMPurify) from 3.3.3 to 3.4.1.
Release notes

Sourced from dompurify's releases.

DOMPurify 3.4.1

  • Fixed an issue with on-handler stripping for HTML-spec-reserved custom element names (font-face, color-profile, missing-glyph, font-face-src, font-face-uri, font-face-format, font-face-name) under permissive CUSTOM_ELEMENT_HANDLING
  • Fixed a case-sensitivity gap in the annotation-xml check that allowed mixed-case variants to bypass the basic-custom-element exclusion in XHTML mode
  • Fixed SANITIZE_NAMED_PROPS repeatedly prefixing already-prefixed id and name values on subsequent sanitization
  • Fixed the IN_PLACE root-node check to explicitly guard against non-string nodeName (DOM-clobbering robustness)
  • Removed a duplicate slot entry from the default HTML attribute allow-list
  • Strengthened the fast-check fuzz harness with explicit XSS invariants, an expanded seed-payload corpus, an additional idempotence property for SANITIZE_NAMED_PROPS, and a negative-control assertion ensuring the invariants actually fire
  • Added regression and pinning tests covering the above fixes and two accepted-behavior contracts (SAFE_FOR_TEMPLATES greedy scrub, hook-added attribute handling)
  • Extended CodeQL analysis to run on 3.x and 2.x maintenance branches

DOMPurify 3.4.0

Most relevant changes:

  • Fixed a problem with FORBID_TAGS not winning over ADD_TAGS, thanks @​kodareef5
  • Fixed several minor problems and typos regarding MathML attributes, thanks @​DavidOliver
  • Fixed ADD_ATTR/ADD_TAGS function leaking into subsequent array-based calls, thanks @​1Jesper1
  • Fixed a missing SAFE_FOR_TEMPLATES scrub in RETURN_DOM path, thanks @​bencalif
  • Fixed a prototype pollution via CUSTOM_ELEMENT_HANDLING, thanks @​trace37labs
  • Fixed an issue with ADD_TAGS function form bypassing FORBID_TAGS, thanks @​eddieran
  • Fixed an issue with ADD_ATTR predicates skipping URI validation, thanks @​christos-eth
  • Fixed an issue with USE_PROFILES prototype pollution, thanks @​christos-eth
  • Fixed an issue leading to possible mXSS via Re-Contextualization, thanks @​researchatfluidattacks and others
  • Fixed an issue with closing tags leading to possible mXSS, thanks @​frevadiscor
  • Fixed a problem with the type dentition patcher after Node version bump
  • Fixed freezing BS runs by reducing the tested browsers array
  • Bumped several dependencies where possible
  • Added needed files for OpenSSF scorecard checks

Published Advisories are here: https://github.com/cure53/DOMPurify/security/advisories?state=published

Commits
  • 5b0cdbb chore: merge main into 3.x for 3.4.1 release (#1301)
  • 09f5911 test: added three more browsers to test setup (OSX, mobile)
  • 5b16e0b Getting 3.x branch ready for 3.4.0 release (#1250)
  • See full diff in compare view
Install script changes

This version adds prepare script that runs during installation. Review the package contents before updating.

records.title: deps: bump hono from 4.12.9 to 4.12.14
records.body:
Bumps [hono](https://github.com/honojs/hono) from 4.12.9 to 4.12.14.
Release notes

Sourced from hono's releases.

v4.12.14

Security fixes

This release includes fixes for the following security issues:

Improper handling of JSX attribute names in hono/jsx SSR

Affects: hono/jsx. Fixes missing validation of JSX attribute names during server-side rendering, which could allow malformed attribute keys to corrupt the generated HTML output and inject unintended attributes or elements. GHSA-458j-xx4x-4375

Other changes

  • fix(aws-lambda): handle invalid header names in request processing (#4883) fa2c74fe

v4.12.13

What's Changed

  • fix(types): infer response type from last handler in app.on 9-/10-handler overloads by @​T4ko0522 in honojs/hono#4865
  • feat(trailing-slash): add skip option by @​yusukebe in honojs/hono#4862
  • feat(cache): add onCacheNotAvailable option by @​yusukebe in honojs/hono#4876

New Contributors

  • @​T4ko0522 made their first contribution in honojs/hono#4865

Full Changelog: https://github.com/honojs/hono/compare/v4.12.12...v4.12.13

v4.12.12

Security fixes

This release includes fixes for the following security issues:

Middleware bypass via repeated slashes in serveStatic

Affects: Serve Static middleware. Fixes a path normalization inconsistency where repeated slashes (//) could bypass route-based middleware protections and allow access to protected static files. GHSA-wmmm-f939-6g9c

Path traversal in toSSG() allows writing files outside the output directory

Affects: toSSG() for Static Site Generation. Fixes a path traversal issue where crafted ssgParams values could write files outside the configured output directory. GHSA-xf4j-xp2r-rqqx

Incorrect IP matching in ipRestriction() for IPv4-mapped IPv6 addresses

Affects: IP Restriction Middleware. Fixes improper handling of IPv4-mapped IPv6 addresses (e.g. ::ffff:127.0.0.1) that could cause allow/deny rules to be bypassed. GHSA-xpcf-pg52-r92g

Missing validation of cookie name on write path in setCookie()

Affects: setCookie(), serialize(), and serializeSigned() from hono/cookie. Fixes missing validation of cookie names on the write path, preventing inconsistent handling between parsing and serialization. GHSA-26pp-8wgv-hjvm

Non-breaking space prefix bypass in cookie name handling in getCookie()

Affects: getCookie() from hono/cookie. Fixes a discrepancy in cookie name handling that could allow attacker-controlled cookies to override legitimate ones and bypass prefix protections. GHSA-r5rp-j6wh-rvv4

... (truncated)

Updates `wrangler` from 4.76.0 to 4.77.0
Release notes

Sourced from wrangler's releases.

wrangler@4.77.0

Minor Changes

  • #13023 593c4db Thanks @​jamesopstad! - Add wrangler versions upload support for the experimental secrets configuration property

    When the new secrets property is defined, wrangler versions upload now validates that all secrets declared in secrets.required are configured on the Worker before the upload succeeds. If any required secrets are missing, the upload fails with a clear error listing which secrets need to be set.

    When secrets is not defined, the existing behavior is unchanged.

    // wrangler.jsonc
{
  "secrets": {
    "required": ["API_KEY", "DB_PASSWORD"]
  }
}

  • #12732 c2e9163 Thanks @​jamesopstad! - Add deploy support for the experimental secrets configuration property

    When the new secrets property is defined, wrangler deploy now validates that all secrets declared in secrets.required are configured on the Worker before the deploy succeeds. If any required secrets are missing, the deploy fails with a clear error listing which secrets need to be set.

    When secrets is not defined, the existing behavior is unchanged.

    // wrangler.jsonc
{
  "secrets": {
    "required": ["API_KEY", "DB_PASSWORD"]
  }
}

Patch Changes

  • #12896 451dae3 Thanks @​petebacondarwin! - fix: Add retry and timeout protection to remote preview API calls

    Remote preview sessions (wrangler dev --remote) now automatically retry transient 5xx API errors (up to 3 attempts with linear backoff) and enforce a 30-second per-request timeout. Previously, a single hung or failed API response during session creation or worker upload could block the dev session reload indefinitely.

  • #12569 379f2a2 Thanks @​MattieTK! - Use qwik add cloudflare-workers instead of qwik add cloudflare-pages for Workers targets

    Both the wrangler autoconfig and C3 Workers template for Qwik were running qwik add cloudflare-pages even when targeting Cloudflare Workers. This caused the wrong adapter directory structure to be scaffolded (adapters/cloudflare-pages/ instead of adapters/cloudflare-workers/), and required post-hoc cleanup of Pages-specific files like _routes.json.

    Qwik now provides a dedicated cloudflare-workers adapter that generates the correct Workers configuration, including wrangler.jsonc with main and assets fields, a public/.assetsignore file, and the correct adapters/cloudflare-workers/vite.config.ts.

    Also adds --skipConfirmation=true to all qwik add invocations so the interactive prompt is skipped in automated contexts.

  • #11899 9a1cf29 Thanks @​hoodmane! - Remove cf-requirements support for Python workers. It hasn't worked with the runtime for a while now.

  • #11800 875da60 Thanks @​southpolesteve! - Add upgrade hint to unexpected configuration field warnings when an update is available

... (truncated)

Updates `typescript-eslint` from 8.56.1 to 8.57.0
Release notes

Sourced from typescript-eslint's releases.

v8.57.0

8.57.0 (2026-03-09)

🚀 Features

  • eslint-plugin: [no-unnecessary-condition] allow literal loop conditions in for/do loops (#12080)

🩹 Fixes

  • eslint-plugin: [strict-void-return] false positives with overloads (#12055)
  • eslint-plugin: handle statically analyzable computed keys in prefer-readonly (#12079)
  • eslint-plugin: guard against negative paramIndex in no-useless-default-assignment (#12077)
  • eslint-plugin: [prefer-promise-reject-errors] add allow TypeOrValueSpecifier to prefer-promise-reject-errors (#12094)
  • eslint-plugin: [no-base-to-string] fix false positive for toString with overloads (#12089)
  • typescript-estree: switch back to use ts.getModifiers() (#12034)
  • typescript-estree: if the template literal is tagged and the text has an invalid escape, cooked will be null (#11355)

❤️ Thank You

  • Brad Zacher @​bradzacher
  • Brian Schlenker @​bschlenk
  • Evyatar Daud @​StyleShit
  • fisker Cheung @​fisker
